label: Provenance, citations & evidence tracking

node.label: Provenance, citations & evidence tracking
node.definition: Provenance and citation tracking attach claims to source identifiers, retrieved passages, transformations, timestamps, and evidence paths.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Provenance, citations & evidence tracking is modeled as depending on External retrieval & ranking, Databases & information retrieval and feeding downstream nodes such as Neural search, RAG & question answering, Memory lifecycle, forgetting & consolidation, Content credentials, signing & provenance.
examples: atlas downstream link to Neural search, RAG & question answering
atlas downstream link to Memory lifecycle, forgetting & consolidation
atlas downstream link to Content credentials, signing & provenance
limitations: Stored or retrieved information can be stale, private, contradictory, inaccessible, or incorrectly ranked at the moment it is needed. Its direct dependencies include External retrieval & ranking, Databases & information retrieval.

dependencies.rationale: In this atlas graph, Provenance, citations & evidence tracking has a `uses` dependency on External retrieval & ranking because External retrieval & ranking is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.

In this atlas graph, Provenance, citations & evidence tracking has a `uses` dependency on Databases & information retrieval because Databases & information retrieval is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.
In this atlas graph, Provenance, citations & evidence tracking has a `retrieves_from` dependency on Retrieval-augmented generation with `common` strength.
